name: Nur Islamic School of Louisville
mission:
Our Mission
Our holistic education aims to provide the environment for the total and balanced development of every student in every sphere of learning –...
theoryOfChange: Holistic education providing total and balanced development (spiritual, moral, imaginative, intellectual, cultural, aesthetic, emotional, physical) directed toward attaining a conscious relationship with God; aims to cultivate lifelong learners with strong and balanced character connected with their Creator and serving humanity.
scoreSummary: NUR ISLAMIC SCHOOL OF LOUISVILLE shows good impact and moderate alignment (minor risk deductions applied).

judge_issues.message: The rationale mentions exceptional financial efficiency and liquidity but does not explicitly address the risk deduction due to the small board size (2 members). This is a key factor affecting the overall score and should be mentioned for completeness.

score_details.score_summary: NUR ISLAMIC SCHOOL OF LOUISVILLE shows good impact and moderate alignment (minor risk deductions applied).

zakat.claim_evidence: The official donation pages and other relevant sections of the Nur Islamic School of Louisville website do not explicitly mention 'zakat' as a type of accepted donation or have a dedicated zakat fund. While they offer a 'Financial Aid Fund' to support students, it is not explicitly stated to be for zakat-eligible recipients. (Source: https://www.nurislamicschool.org/donate.html)

alignment.explanation: The school serves a <cite id="9">critical need for Islamic education</cite> in Kentucky, though it lacks specific features like a <cite id="16">dedicated zakat-eligibility policy</cite>.

credibility.explanation: The school is a long-standing institution <cite id="1">founded in 1999</cite> with <cite id="12">verified CISNA accreditation</cite>. However, credibility is tempered by a <cite id="13">board of only 2 members</cite>, which limits independent oversight.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the school should <cite id="13">expand its board of directors</cite> to include more independent members and publish annual impact reports.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who value long-term investment in Islamic educational infrastructure and prioritize charities with zero overhead and high liquidity.

Interested in holistic development (spiritual and academic)
giving_considerations: Donations are considered Sadaqah, not Zakat

High reliance on tuition and program fees alongside donations

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: The school holds <cite id="12">Cognia and CISNA accreditations</cite>, which require external validation of educational standards and outcomes.

impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Accreditation ensures the school meets rigorous national standards for curriculum and student support.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With <cite id="10">29% annual revenue growth</cite>, the school is expanding but maintains high reserves to manage its growth.

strengths.detail: The school directs <cite id="6">1.0% of its expenses toward program services</cite>, ensuring donor funds are used exclusively for educational delivery.
The organization has achieved a <cite id="10">3-year revenue CAGR of 29.0%</cite>, growing from $1.68 million in 2021 to nearly $2.8 million in 2023.
With <cite id="8">12.2 months of working capital</cite>, the school maintains a robust financial safety net compared to industry standards.

strengths_deep_dive: Nur Islamic School of Louisville distinguishes itself through extreme fiscal discipline. According to its <cite id="5">2023 financial filings</cite>, the school managed to generate <cite id="11">$1,291,282 in program service revenue</cite> alongside <cite id="11">$1,505,486 in contributions</cite>. Despite this growth, it maintains <cite id="7">zero fundraising costs</cite>, a rare metric among private educational institutions. This efficiency is paired with academic rigor, as evidenced by its <cite id="12">accreditation from Cognia and CISNA</cite>, which validates the quality of its holistic educational model.
rich_narrative.summary: Founded in <cite id="1">1999</cite>, Nur Islamic School of Louisville (NISL) serves as a cornerstone for <cite id="2">holistic K-12 education</cite> in the Louisville, Kentucky region. The institution integrates <cite id="3">divine revelation with rational knowledge</cite>, offering a curriculum that spans from an <cite id="4">Early Childhood Center to high school</cite>. Financially, the school operates with a remarkably lean structure, reporting <cite id="5">$2,796,768 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 tax year. A defining characteristic of its fiscal management is a <cite id="6">100.0% program expense ratio</cite>, indicating that all functional expenses are dedicated directly to its educational mission. Furthermore, the organization demonstrates exceptional fundraising efficiency, spending <cite id="7">$0.00 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions. This financial stability is bolstered by <cite id="8">12.2 months of working capital</cite>, providing a significant buffer for long-term operational sustainability. Beyond standard academics, the school provides specialized instruction in <cite id="9">Quran, Arabic, and Islamic Studies</cite>, ensuring a culturally and religiously grounded environment for its students.
